data_class: LIVE (current production DB state on 2026-06-17, NOT the frozen seal). These counts move as the system runs; they are a snapshot, not a sealed artifact. Distinct from the seal anchor numbers used elsewhere.

real_learners_distinct.sql: SELECT count(DISTINCT user_id) FROM experiment_trajectories WHERE traffic_type IN ('human','live')
real_learners_distinct.note: 'live' (721) is the synthetic-cohort live-replay traffic class; 'human' (54) is genuine human-driven traffic. Both are real-learner (non-research-batch) classes per the trajectory-authority model.

real_learner_interactions.sql: SELECT count(*) FROM experiment_trajectories WHERE traffic_type IN ('human','live')

distinct_experiment_run_id.sql_clean: SELECT count(DISTINCT experiment_run_id) FROM experiment_trajectories WHERE experiment_run_id LIKE 'run-%'
distinct_experiment_run_id.caveat: RAW = 24722 distinct experiment_run_id strings, but MOST are NOT real run identifiers: the 'live' traffic class stores a per-learner composite key in this column of the form 'live::run-<uuid>::ex_<dataset>_<learner>', inflating the distinct count. Only 77 distinct values are clean canonical 'run-<uuid>' experiment runs. Report 77 as the number of actual experiment runs and disclose the 24722 raw inflation.
